name: subto

metadata.name: subto
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A package to make life easier when submitting jobs to schedulers

documentation:
`subto` is a package to make life easier when submitting jobs to schedulers



Please see the `subto` documentation for more details.

modules.name: subto.__version__
documentation: IT IS AUTOMATICALLY CHANGED BY python-semantic-release

modules.name: subto
documentation: subto is a package for using generating submission scripts

classes.name: subto.job.Job
documentation: Abstract class on which all submission/job scripts classes are based.

classes.name: subto.job.SlurmJob

methods.name: subto.job.SlurmJob.write_script
documentation:
Writes submission script to file

Parameters
----------
verbose: bool, default True
    If True, jobscript location is written to screen

Class to generate Slurm submission/job scripts


Submission script format is 


...............

SCHEDULER BLOCK

...............

CONTENT BLOCK
...............


Attributes
----------
job_file: str
    Submission script name
job_name: str
    --job-name value, default is taken from job_file
account: str
    --account value
partition: str
    --partition value
error: str
    --error value
output: str
    --output value
mem_per_cpu: str
    --mem-per-cpu value
cpus_per_task: str
    --cpus-per-task value
ntasks_per_node: str
    --ntasks-per-node value
ntasks: str
    --ntasks value
nodes: str
    --nodes value
signal: str
    --signal value
qos: str
    --qos value
gpus_per_node: str
    --gpus-per-node
time: str
    --time value
content_block: str
    Commands to include in jobscript after scheduler block

functions.name: subto.utils.cprint
documentation:
Prints colorised output to screen

Parameters
----------
string: str
    String to print
color: str {red, green, yellow, blue, magenta, cyan, white}
    String name of color

Returns
-------
None

functions.name: subto.utils.get_opt_coords
documentation:
Extracts coordinates from orca optimisation cycle

Parameters
----------
file_name: str
    Name of file to check

Returns
-------
list[str]
    Labels
list[float]
    Coordinates (3,n_atoms)
bool
    True if stationary point found

functions.name: subto.utils.get_input_section
documentation: Extracts Input section from orca output file

functions.name: subto.utils.check_envvar
documentation:
Checks specified environment variable has been defined, exits program if
variable is not defined

Parameters
----------
var_str : str
    String name of environment variable

Returns
-------
None

functions.name: subto.utils.get_envvar
documentation:
Gets specified environment variable
If undefined then returns empty string

Parameters
----------
var_str : str
    String name of environment variable

Returns
-------
str
    Value of environment variable, or empty is not defined

functions.name: subto.utils.flatten_recursive
documentation:
Flatten a list of lists recursively.

Parameters
----------
to_flat: list

Returns
-------
list
    Input list flattened to a single list
